Name: adaptive_latency_aware_quantization
Title: Adaptive Latency-Aware Mixed Precision Quantization for Low-Power Edge Devices
Short Hypothesis: The integration of adaptive mixed-precision quantization with runtime latency management can significantly enhance the inference efficiency of deep learning models on resource-constrained edge devices without extensive retraining, thereby optimizing for both accuracy and performance.
Related Work: While existing methods focus on adaptive mixed-precision quantization, they often overlook the critical role of runtime latency management for edge applications. Techniques like RAMP and FlexiQ offer promising insights but do not fully address the dynamic nature of edge workloads or the need for real-time adaptability. This proposal builds on their foundational ideas but introduces a novel framework that simultaneously manages quantization and runtime inference latency, specifically tailored for edge deployments.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel framework for Adaptive Latency-Aware Mixed Precision Quantization (ALAMPQ) designed for low-power edge devices. Current mixed-precision quantization methods often apply uniform bit-widths across model layers, leading to suboptimal performance trade-offs during inference. Our framework dynamically adjusts the precision of model layers in real-time based on the current workload and latency requirements, achieved through a lightweight monitoring mechanism that assesses input characteristics and adjusts quantization parameters accordingly. This approach aims to maintain high inference accuracy while minimizing latency and memory overhead. We will validate our method on various deep learning tasks, comparing performance against state-of-the-art quantization techniques. Our framework promises to enhance the deployment of deep learning models on resource-constrained hardware, effectively bridging the gap between model performance and practical usability in real-world applications.
Experiments: 1. Implement the ALAMPQ framework on a set of benchmark models (e.g., MobileNet, ResNet) and establish a baseline using standard mixed-precision quantization methods.
2. Conduct experiments to compare the performance of ALAMPQ against static mixed-precision quantization methods under different load conditions and inputs to assess latency and accuracy trade-offs.
3. Measure the real-time adaptability of ALAMPQ by introducing variable input scenarios and recording latency, memory usage, and accuracy metrics.
4. Perform ablation studies to isolate the effects of different components of the framework (e.g., monitoring mechanism, adjustment algorithms) on overall performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the reliability of the real-time monitoring mechanism, which may introduce overhead if not optimized. Additionally, the effectiveness of ALAMPQ may vary across different model architectures and tasks, necessitating broad testing. The complexity of implementation may also pose challenges in reproducibility and generalization to new model types.
